content: $\log \left(\log _{a b} a+\frac{1}{\log _b a b}\right)$ is $($ where $a b \neq 1)$
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \log \left(\log _{a b} a+\frac{1}{\log _b a b}\right) \\ & =\log \left(\log _{a b} a+\log _{a b} b\right)\left\{\because \log _m n=\frac{1}{\log _n m}\right\} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &= \log \left(\log _{a b}(a b)\right) \\ &\left\{\because \log _b m+\log _b n=\log _b(m n)\right\} \end{aligned}$$

$=\log (1)=0$

content: If $a, b$ and $c$ are distinct positive numbers, not equal to unity. Such that $a b c=1$, then the value of $\log _b a \cdot \log _c a+\log _c b$ $\cdot \log _a b+\log _a c \log _b c$ is
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text { } \because a b c=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \log a+\log b+\log c=0 \\ & \text { that means, } \\ & \log ^3 a+\log ^3 b+\log ^3 c=3 \log a \cdot \log b \cdot \log c \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\log ^2 a}{\log b \cdot \log c}+\frac{\log ^2 b}{\log a \cdot \log c}+\frac{\log ^2 c}{\log a \cdot \log b}=3 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\log a \cdot \log a}{\log b \cdot \log c}+\frac{\log b \cdot \log b}{\log a \cdot \log c}+\frac{\log c \cdot \log c}{\log a \cdot \log b}=3 \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{\log _b a}{\log _a c}+\frac{\log _c b}{\log _b a}+\frac{\log _a c}{\log _c b}=3 \\ & \Rightarrow \log _b a \cdot \log _c a+\log _c b \cdot \log _a b \\ & +\log _a c \cdot \log _b c=3 \end{aligned}$$
